steps.description: Rust integration test for Lock flow
steps.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test sequence_lock -- --nocapture
steps.expected: Lock created in DB, SR0 computed, L1 lockWithSR0 submitted

steps.description: Playwright integration spec for Consumer Lock
steps.command: cd src/frontend/web && npx playwright test e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts --reporter=json
steps.expected: UI lock creation produces success state and DB row

steps.description: Verify lock row was created and pk_dilithium populated
steps.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"

steps.description: Verify totalLocked increased on Sepolia Vault

acceptance_criteria: A new locks row exists with status=locked and Dilithium pk len 1952
No silent fallback or hardcoded address in the trace

observations: exit_code is 1, indicating test execution failed
stdout_excerpt is empty; no test output captured
stderr_excerpt shows only compilation output with no test execution trace
No 'test result:' marker found in any output
No panic or assertion failure details visible in stderr
Compilation completed successfully (many crates compiled), but test did not run or crashed before producing output

issues.title: Test execution failed with no output
issues.detail: cargo test exited with code 1 but produced no stdout and only compilation logs in stderr. The test binary likely failed to execute, panicked during setup, or crashed with output suppressed. Cannot verify any part of the expected behavior: 'Lock created in DB, SR0 computed, L1 lockWithSR0 submitted'.

issues.title: Missing test result assertion
issues.detail: Expected to find 'test result: ok' or similar success marker in stdout, but stdout is empty. This indicates the test either did not complete, was skipped, or panicked before completion.

observations: Playwright test execution failed due to missing '@playwright/test' module
npm attempted to install playwright@1.59.1 but dependencies were not properly resolved
Test spec: e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts could not be loaded or executed
Expected behavior: UI lock creation produces success state and DB row

issues.title: Missing Playwright test dependency
issues.detail: The '@playwright/test' module could not be resolved in the playwright.config.ts. This indicates that either node_modules are not installed in src/frontend/web, or the Playwright installation is incomplete. The npx fallback installation of playwright@1.59.1 did not include the required test package.

issues.title: Frontend dependencies not installed before test run
issues.detail: The test environment requires npm install (or yarn/pnpm install) to be run in src/frontend/web before executing Playwright tests. The npx on-demand installation strategy is insufficient for config file resolution.

observations: stdout_excerpt is empty — no rows returned
expected one row with status=locked and pk_dilithium length=1952

issues.title: No lock record found
issues.detail: Expected one row from locks table ordered by created_at DESC LIMIT 1, but query returned zero rows. This indicates either the locks table is empty or the Consumer Lock creation failed.

issues.title: Missing pk_dilithium cryptographic material
issues.detail: Cannot verify pk_dilithium length because no lock record exists. The cryptographic write path may be broken.

observations: RPC endpoint returned HTTP 522 (Connection timed out)
Cloudflare reports origin server (rpc.sepolia.org) did not complete the request
Address 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 is a legitimate L1Vault contract

issues.title: RPC Infrastructure Outage
issues.detail: The Sepolia RPC endpoint is experiencing connectivity issues. Cloudflare 522 error indicates the origin server is not responding within timeout window. This is an infrastructure issue, not a contract state failure.

quality_findings.title: Cross-layer silent failure: backend test crashed with no output AND DB is empty — lock was never created
quality_findings.detail: The backend test exited with code 1 and produced zero stdout. The DB layer independently confirmed zero rows in the locks table. Together these prove the Consumer Lock creation path failed entirely — not merely a test-harness flake. Because the DB query itself succeeded (exit 0) but returned no rows, the failure is in the application code or its setup, not in the query. The L1 call could not be verified due to RPC outage (HTTP 522), but the DB emptiness alone is sufficient to conclude that SR0 was never computed and `lockWithSR0` was never submitted.
evidence: backend: exit_code=1, stdout_excerpt='', stderr shows only compilation output — no test execution trace at all
db: exit_code=0 (psql ran fine), stdout_excerpt='' — zero rows returned from `SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1`
l1: HTTP 522 from rpc.sepolia.org — totalLocked() unverifiable, consistent with no lock having been submitted
SEQUENCES.md §1 requires: Lock created in DB, SR0 computed, L1 lockWithSR0 submitted — none of these can be confirmed

quality_findings.title: Backend test binary never executed — likely panic or link failure in test setup, not a test assertion failure
quality_findings.detail: A normal cargo test failure (assertion mismatch, timeout, etc.) still prints 'test result: FAILED' to stdout. Here stdout is completely empty despite compilation succeeding. This matches one of three silent-failure patterns: (a) the test binary panicked during global/async setup before any test ran, (b) a background task swallowed an error via `Ok(())` or similar and the test harness received a non-zero exit from the runtime, or (c) a `unwrap_or_default()` / silent fallback silently corrupted state before the first assertion. All three are on the Quantum Shield silent-failure watch-list (items 5, 3, 4). The duration of 300 032 ms (exactly 5 minutes) strongly suggests a timeout — the test binary hung waiting for a resource (DB connection pool, RabbitMQ, or an L1 RPC call that also timed out) and was killed by the test runner.
evidence: backend: duration_ms=300032 — exactly 5-minute wall-clock timeout; cargo test default timeout is not 5 min, so an external runner killed the process
backend: exit_code=1 with empty stdout — no 'running N tests', no 'test result:' line
Compilation succeeded fully ('Compiling' lines end normally); the binary was produced but hung or panicked in setup
Known silent-failure pattern #5: `Ok(())` swallowing background task errors — a background Ethereum/RPC task timing out could cause this exact symptom

quality_findings.title: L1 RPC outage masks whether vault address regression exists — cannot clear item #1 from silent-failure checklist
quality_findings.detail: The vault address `0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260` is the correct known-good address and was correctly used in the cast call. However, because the RPC returned HTTP 522, we have no confirmation that `totalLocked()` is non-zero. Combined with the DB being empty, it is impossible to rule out that a hardcoded wrong address or `0x...0002` placeholder (silent-failure items #1 and #2) is present in the backend code path and the lock call silently went to the wrong contract. This must be verified by a code search before this sequence is marked green.
evidence: l1: `cast call 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 'totalLocked()(uint256)' --rpc-url $L1_RPC_URL` → HTTP 522, exit_code=1
db: zero rows — backend never wrote a lock row, meaning the L1 submission step was never reached, but the address used in the submission code is unverified
Silent-failure history items #1 (`0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260` is the only correct address) and #2 (`0x...0002` placeholder) require positive L1 confirmation, which is unavailable

quality_findings.title: Frontend test environment is broken — `@playwright/test` not installed, lock UI flow completely unverified
quality_findings.detail: The frontend layer failed before running a single test because `@playwright/test` is not installed in `src/frontend/web/node_modules`. This is not merely a CI inconvenience: it means the entire UI lock-creation flow (button state, wallet connection, success/error display) is unverified for this sequence run. Critically, it also means frontend-side bugs such as `SAMPLE_PROPOSALS` hardcoding (silent-failure item #12) or `wallet_address = 'caller'` (item #7) in the lock path cannot be detected by this layer.
evidence: frontend: `Error: Cannot find module '@playwright/test'` — requireStack shows playwright.config.ts:1 as the entry point
frontend: exit_code=1, duration_ms=6087 — failed at config load, zero tests executed

quality_findings.title: pk_dilithium cryptographic material never written — Dilithium key storage path may be silently broken
quality_findings.detail: The DB query specifically checks `length(pk_dilithium)` and the expected value is 1952 bytes (Dilithium-3 public key). The table is empty, so we cannot confirm the key was generated or stored. However, this column is a known regression target: if `hex_to_bytes32_or_zero` (silent-failure item #4) or `unwrap_or_default()` (item #3) is used anywhere in the Dilithium key serialization path, a bad key would be stored as zeroes rather than raising an error. With zero rows the failure is upstream, but when the write path is fixed, this specific column must be asserted to be exactly 1952 bytes — not merely non-null and not zero-filled.
evidence: db: zero rows from `SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1` — pk_dilithium unverifiable
Silent-failure items #3 and #4 specifically target hex/byte parse errors being silently swallowed in cryptographic material paths
Spec §1 requires pk_dilithium to be present and valid in the DB row before L1 submission proceeds

fixer_proposals.title: Add startup-probe timeout and propagate connection errors instead of hanging in test setup
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings 'Backend test binary never executed' and 'Backend layer exceeds 5-minute threshold' both point to the test binary hanging for exactly 300 s before being killed — a classic symptom of a resource acquisition (DB pool, RabbitMQ, RPC) that blocks forever with no timeout. The fix adds an explicit, short connect-timeout on the test-harness setup helpers and converts any `unwrap_or_default()` / silent-`Ok(())` returns in setup into hard errors that produce visible output before the timeout kills the process. This directly addresses silent-failure patterns #3, #4, and #5 from the watch-list.

fixer_proposals.title: Remove silent unwrap_or_default in DB pool creation and propagate errors with context
fixer_proposals.rationale: Finding 'Backend test binary never executed — likely panic or link failure in test setup' and silent-failure watch-list items #3/#4 identify unwrap_or_default() as a root-cause pattern. The pool.rs hunk in P1 already removes it; this proposal handles the downstream call-sites that discard the Result by matching on it silently. Each call-site must now use `?` or an explicit expect with a human-readable message so that a misconfigured DATABASE_URL produces visible test output rather than a silent hang-then-kill.

fixer_proposals.title: Assert pk_dilithium is exactly 1952 bytes and non-zero-filled after lock creation
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings 'Missing pk_dilithium cryptographic material' and 'pk_dilithium cryptographic material never written' (silent-failure items #3/#4) require that once the write path is unblocked by P1/P2, the test must hard-assert both the byte length (1952, Dilithium-3) and that the stored bytes are not all-zero (which would indicate a silent hex-parse fallback). Without this assertion the test can pass with a zero-filled column and the regression would go undetected.
fixer_proposals.unified_diff:
--- a/src/api/api/tests/sequence_e2e_test.rs
+++ b/src/api/api/tests/sequence_e2e_test.rs
@@ -30,6 +30,30 @@ async fn sequence_lock() {
     // After triggering lock creation, verify the DB row:
     let row = sqlx::query!(
-        "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) AS pk_len FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1"
+        r#"
+        SELECT
+            lock_id,
+            status,
+            length(pk_dilithium)            AS pk_len,
+            pk_dilithium = repeat('\x00', length(pk_dilithium))::bytea AS is_zero_filled
+        FROM locks
+        ORDER BY created_at DESC
+        LIMIT 1
+        "#
     )
     .fetch_one(&pool)
     .await
-    .unwrap_or_default();
+    .expect("Expected one row in locks table — lock was never written to DB");
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        row.status.as_deref(),
+        Some("locked"),
+        "Lock row status must be 'locked', got {:?}",
+        row.status
+    );
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        row.pk_len,
+        Some(1952),
+        "pk_dilithium must be exactly 1952 bytes (Dilithium-3 public key), got {:?} bytes",
+        row.pk_len
+    );
+
+    assert!(
+        !row.is_zero_filled.unwrap_or(true),
+        "pk_dilithium is all-zero bytes — hex/byte parse silently fell back to zeroes"
+    );
 
     // existing assertions continue below

fixer_proposals.title: Install @playwright/test (not playwright) as a dev dependency and add pre-test install step
fixer_proposals.rationale: Findings 'Missing Playwright test dependency' and 'Frontend dependencies not installed before test run' show that npx installed the wrong package (playwright instead of @playwright/test). The package.json must declare @playwright/test as a devDependency so that npm install populates node_modules correctly, and the CI/test runner script must run npm ci before npx playwright test.

fixer_proposals.title: Investigation required — vault address and L1 submission code path cannot be verified without RPC connectivity
fixer_proposals.rationale: Finding 'L1 RPC outage masks whether vault address regression exists' (silent-failure items #1/#2) requires a positive confirmation that the hardcoded vault address 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 — and not a 0x...0002 placeholder — is used in the Rust submission call. This cannot be verified programmatically while rpc.sepolia.org returns HTTP 522. A code-search grep for both addresses in src/api/api and src/contracts/l1 must be performed manually and the result recorded before this sequence is marked green. No automated patch can substitute for this check.

final_verdict.summary: The Consumer Lock sequence failed across three of four layers with no single fixable root-cause patch covering all issues. The backend test binary timed out at exactly 300 s (killed by the runner) without emitting any test output, and the DB independently confirms zero lock rows were written — meaning the full creation path (SR0 computation, Dilithium key storage, L1 submission) never executed. The frontend is broken due to missing node_modules (@playwright/test not installed), and the L1 layer is unverifiable due to a Cloudflare 522 RPC outage on rpc.sepolia.org; while the L1 failure is infrastructure-only and would normally permit a downgrade, the backend hang and empty DB are code-level failures that cannot be auto-resolved. No fixer proposals were produced, so there is nothing to apply.
must_fix_before_merge: Backend test binary hangs for 5 minutes and is killed — identify and fix blocking resource acquisition (DB pool, RabbitMQ, or L1 RPC call) in test setup or add explicit timeouts
Zero rows in locks table — Consumer Lock creation path is entirely non-functional; lock row, SR0, and pk_dilithium are never written
pk_dilithium storage path unverified — once write path is restored, assert column is exactly 1952 bytes and not zero-filled (guard against unwrap_or_default / hex_to_bytes32_or_zero silent failures)
Frontend node_modules not installed — @playwright/test missing; run npm install in src/frontend/web before any Playwright test execution

L1 vault address correctness unverifiable — RPC outage prevents confirming totalLocked() > 0 and that no hardcoded wrong address (0x...0002 placeholder) is present in backend submission path; code-search required as compensating control
Backend test timeout suggests silent-swallowed error in async setup — audit for Ok(()) returns in background tasks and ensure all setup errors propagate and fail-fast
unresolved_questions: What specific resource is the backend test binary blocking on during setup that causes it to hang for exactly 300 s before being killed — DB connection pool exhaustion, RabbitMQ unavailability, or an outbound L1 RPC call in test initialization?

Is the L1 vault submission address in the backend code actually 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260 (correct) or a placeholder — cannot be confirmed from test output alone and RPC outage prevents runtime verification?

The db layer reports exit_code=0 (pass) but returned zero rows — is this a pre-existing empty test database or evidence that a prior run partially succeeded and cleaned up, or has the locks table never been populated in this environment?
